zh.lifeSummary: 當Comtesse Pétronille de Comminges et de Bigorre於1180年出生在法國米迪-列內斯可明格斯時，她的父親Comte Bernard  IV De Comminges28歲，母親Comtesse Stéphanie de Bigorre25歲。她於1196年6月1日與Gaston VI de Montcada Vicomte de Béarn在法國豪-加洛姆斯特結婚。她去世於1251年11月3日在法國豪-列內斯享年71歲，埋葬在法國豪-列內斯。

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

English: variant of   Cummings  .

variants.name: Cummings

nameMeanings.name: et
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Heang, Heng, Chheng, Eng, Kheng, Khun, Sang, Seng, Sung.  Vietnamese Anh, Hoa, Huong, Mang, Thao, Thong.  Spanish Angelita, Arturo, Enrique, Guillermo, Renato, Rosario.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 戴, see   Dai   1.
Vietnamese (Tề): from the Chinese surname 齊, see Qi 1.

variants.name: Dai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Cheng, Chi, Jin, Weimin, Xing, Zheng, Chung, Dong, Hong, Meng, Ning, Peihua.  Vietnamese Hung, Quang, Thanh, Anh, Cuong, Dai, Khanh, Lam Van, Minh, Mui, Nghia, Ngoc.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 戴, meaning ‘respect, support’ in Chinese: (i) from Dai (戴), the posthumous title of Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). (ii) from Dai (戴), the name of an ancient state (located in Henan province). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng in 713  BC , the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted in place of another Chinese surname, Yin (殷), because they shared a similar meaning, ‘deep feeling’, in ancient Chinese.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 代, meaning ‘historical periods’ in Chinese: (i) from Dai (代), the name of an ancient state (located in Hebei province). After it was annexed by the state of Zhao during the Warring States period (475–221  BC ), the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (ii) traced back to the Dai (代) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Pétronille
meanings: An old female name; an Italian diminutive formed from Pietro, i.e. Peter.

en.lifeSummary: When Comtesse Pétronille de Comminges et de Bigorre was born in 1180, in Comminges, Midi-Pyrénées, France, her father, Comte Bernard  IV De Comminges, was 28 and her mother, Comtesse Stéphanie de Bigorre, was 25. She married Gaston VI de Montcada Vicomte de Béarn on 1 June 1196, in Muret, Haute-Garonne, France. She died on 3 November 1251, in Hautes-Pyrénées, France, at the age of 71, and was buried in Hautes-Pyrénées, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Comtesse Pétronille de Comminges et de Bigorre est née en 1180, à Comminges, Midi-Pyrénées, France, son père, Comte Bernard  IV De Comminges, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Comtesse Stéphanie de Bigorre, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Gaston VI de Montcada Vicomte de Béarn le 1 juin 1196, à Muret, Haute-Garonne, France. Elle est décédée le 3 novembre 1251, à Hautes-Pyrénées, France, à l’âge de 71 ans, et a été enterrée à Hautes-Pyrénées, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Comtesse Pétronille de Comminges et de Bigorre 1180 in Comminges, Midi-Pyrénées, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Comte Bernard  IV De Comminges 28 und ihre Mutter Comtesse Stéphanie de Bigorre 25. Sie hat am 1. Juni 1196 in Muret, Haute-Garonne, Frankreich Gaston VI de Montcada Vicomte de Béarn geheiratet. Sie starb am 3. November 1251 im Alter von 71 Jahren in Hautes-Pyrénées, Frankreich und wurde in Hautes-Pyrénées, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Comtesse Pétronille de Comminges et de Bigorre è nata il nel 1180 nel Comminges, Midi-Pyrénées, Francia, sua padre, Comte Bernard  IV De Comminges, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Comtesse Stéphanie de Bigorre, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato Gaston VI de Montcada Vicomte de Béarn il 1 giugno 1196, nel Muret, Haute-Garonne, Francia. Morì il 3 novembre 1251, nel Hautes-Pyrénées, Francia, all'età di 71 anni, e fu sepolta a Hautes-Pyrénées, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Comtesse Pétronille de Comminges et de Bigorreが1180年にフランスミディ-プイルeンeスコンミンジェズで生まれた時，父Comte Bernard  IV De Commingesは28歳，母Comtesse Stéphanie de Bigorreは25歳であった。1196年6月1日にフランスハウテ-ガーオンムレトでGaston VI de Montcada Vicomte de Béarnと結婚した。彼女は1251年11月3日にフランスハウテス-プイルeンeスで71歳で死去し，フランスハウテス-プイルeンeスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Comtesse Pétronille de Comminges et de Bigorre(이)가 1180년 프랑스 미디-p이ee스 코밍스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Comte Bernard  IV De Comminges(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Comtesse Stéphanie de Bigorre(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1196년 6월 1일 프랑스 하테-가니 머트에서 Gaston VI de Montcada Vicomte de Béarn(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1251년 11월 3일 프랑스 하츠-p이ee스에서 71세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 하츠-p이ee스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Comtesse Pétronille de Comminges et de Bigorre nasceu em 1180, em Comminges, Midi-Pyrénées, França, seu pai, Comte Bernard  IV De Comminges, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Comtesse Stéphanie de Bigorre, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Gaston VI de Montcada Vicomte de Béarn em 1 de junho de 1196, em Muret, Haute-Garonne, França. Ela faleceu em 3 de novembro de 1251, em Hautes-Pyrénées, França, com 71 anos, e foi sepultada em Hautes-Pyrénées, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Comtesse Pétronille de Comminges et de Bigorre родилась в 1180 г. в местности коммингесь, миди-пирeнeесь, Франция, её отцу по имени Comte Bernard  IV De Comminges было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Comtesse Stéphanie de Bigorre, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Gaston VI de Montcada Vicomte de Béarn 1 июня 1196 г. в местности мурет, хауте-гаронн, Франция. Она умерла 3 ноября 1251 г. в местности хаутес-пирeнeесь, Франция в возрасте 71 год и была похоронена в местности хаутес-пирeнeесь, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Comtesse Pétronille de Comminges et de Bigorre nació en 1180, en Comminges, Mediodía-Pirineos, Francia, su papá, Comte Bernard  IV De Comminges, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Comtesse Stéphanie de Bigorre, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Gaston VI de Montcada Vicomte de Béarn el 1 de junio de 1196, en Muret, Haute-Garonne, Francia. Murió el 3 de noviembre de 1251, en Hautes-Pyrénées, Francia, a la edad de 71 años, y fue sepultada en Hautes-Pyrénées, Francia.
